The risk assessment of the proposed recommendations 73 Conclusion of a final qualification paper 76 Reference list 78 Introduction The  given theme for the final qualification  paper was  introduced   due to  the fact, that the contemporary world faces  many   essential  challenges related to the  environmental degradation   and social issues . In fact, ecological problems  are increasing their scale   at a tremendous pace .   Moreover , the  trend of a conscious consumption  is  spreading  in the society   that  significantly influences  conventional strategies and operations of the companies .  To lessen the negative environmental  footprint  and to  enhance the  living conditions of a  society ,   the enterprises  worldwide change their direction toward a sustainable  development .   So that, nowadays the businesses tend to make an emphasis on the development of the value creation  based on  safeguarding the environment  and  climate change , which is   aimed to  improve  the  societal well-being .    The  organizations shift  their affairs  in  a new  way that  integrates the concept of the shared value with the core business of the company.  However, it is essential not only to   be able to respond on the sustainability issues , but also  to m aintain the profitability level.  To achieve that  the enterprises should modify a traditional way of doing business and “adopt innovative business models, which means to develop new architectures of organizational value creation, delivery, and capture”  ( Florian et al., 2016). One of the glo bal corporations that works  thoroughly  on  the sustainability program,  functioning under the  ecological  principles  in its  production from its foundation is IKEA company.   IKEA   constantly develops  innovative  approaches  in its business model , which is based on sustainable development .  The company  operates in the  furnishing  industry that requires a  wide utilization of   natural  wooden  resources, so that  IKEA   always  elaborate s  and improve s  the ways how to use and recycle the se resources  more eff iciently , meaning that the company   takes care about environmental issues.  In addition to this,  IKEA   successfully  implements the program s aimed to support societal challenges . Furthermore,  it  introduces  the  improved  tactic s  to inspire  the change in customers’ minds, so that  more and more  people will be able  to become the part of global projects,  bringing the valuable impact for the  thriving  future . Nowadays, IKEA is one  of the companies that prosperous ly  introduces  the circular economy  principles in   its  production  and  in  daily operations.  IKEA sustainable principles  expands  broadly  in Russia, covering the manufacturing ,  offices,   and  stores functioning  (IKEA, n.d.) .   Managerial Problem Identification Based on the Sustainability Review that was hold in Russia at the beginning of 2020, IKEA Russia has received 91 out of 100 scores for its successful implementation of initiatives toward the sustainable development. Those significant results indicated that IKEA Russia has taken the highest scores for last 3 years, becoming the best sustainability company in Russian market. However, according to the insights provided by the sustainable development managers of IKEA Russia, these results could be even higher, if the company introduced bigger number of sustainability-oriented projects. IKEA Russia is expected to expand the scope of sustainable development initiatives according to the multinational sustainability strategy of IKEA named “People and Planet Positive”. The barriers for the sustainable projects’ expansion are primarily connected to the peculiarities of Russian market. So that it can be stated that IKEA Russia has the  managerial problem  that is the challenges with the implementation of the sustainable initiatives in Russia, which are demanded by the international IKEA strategy. As a matter of fact, these challenges are associated majorly with the peculiarities of Russian market. The identified managerial problem illustrates the relevance of the chosen topic for the current paper, since the sustainability is highly urgent direction for the development in IKEA business agenda. In addition to this, the multinational sustainability strategy requires each IKEA subsidiary to adjust IKEA sustainability ambitions until 2030. Personally,  for me, the problem of IKEA from the one hand is a changeling one,  due to the fact that   the company  has already implemented many successful projects  in Russia ,  so that it would be hard to propose better solutions. B ut from  the other hand,  it is qui te interesting to investigate the p ossible gaps and elaborate valid innovations  in the field of sustainability.   It is important to mention that the managerial problem   of IKEA  is strengthened by the  peculiarities  of Russian market , which were indicated during the interview.  The major ones are  illustrated  below. Firstly, there is a lack of infrastructure, which can support and accelerate the sustainability initiatives of the company around the cities. It means that the speed of the projects aimed toward sustainable development realization is lower in comparison with other countries. The problem is connected to the low number of competent contactors and partners, such as recycling organizations, who are able to provide the necessary support to achieve the strategic goal of IKEA that is to inspire more than 100 million citizens to live more sustainable lives. Another  trait in Russia  is that t here  is a  low level of  understanding  of  global problems importance   among Russian citizens . T herefore, customers’ involvement in sustainability programs is not considerable, since, basing on the internal review of IKEA ,  the population tends not to believe in the  effectiveness of the sustainability  projects.   The acceptance rate of  IKEA  sustainable programs in Moscow and other big cities of Russia  (excluding Saint Petersburg)   is  low comparing to the figures of other countries. Nevertheless, the level of customers’ participation in environmental projects in Saint Petersburg during 2018-2020 was growing comparing to the performance of previous years. It illustrates, that there is a good tendency for the development of s ustainable  initiatives in Russia n market .  For instance , a ccording to the  internal  survey of IKEA Russia among clients, they did not use or just rarely used the separate waste collection “stations”  (nearby IKEA stores) , since these stations  were situated quite far  from their apartments. However, the expansion of  these types of stations around many city areas is  complicated  issue,  since the company has to invest considerable number of financial resources and it  also  needs to obtain the official permits to gather and keep the collected waste. Thus, the goal of the paper will be primarily connected to the development of the solution for the identified manage rial problem within IKEA Russia, based on the thorough analysis.  The paper goal and objectives So that ,   according to  the managerial problem   stated above,  t he goal  of the  qualification paper  is to make the analysis of the  sustainable initiatives of IKEA Russia  and to  elaborate   recommendations for  the  development and implementation of   sustainable   innovations  for   the company, so that it can correspond the demands from international IKEA strategy.  Therefore,  the object  of the research is  an IKEA international sustainability strategy  for 2030   and  the subject  of the research is  the adjustment of the initiatives of international sustainability strategy of IKEA in Russian market. In order to realize the goal specified above, it is necessary to  cover  the following  objectives : To illustrate and justify the choice of the viable managerial instruments, which are applicable for the resolution of the indicated problem; to examine the strategic positioning and the sustainable orientation of the company;  to reveal the peculiarities of sustainable development program of IKEA and its initiatives globally and in Russia; to make an analysis of the current sustainable practices through the prism of company’s value chain; to perform the sustainability-based analysis of the external environment with the application to the furniture retail industry; to evaluate the current practices of IKEA Russia through the prism of sustainable innovations and identify the gaps; to elaborate sustainability SWOT-analysis to justify the need for  sustainable  innovations; to reveal the best sustainable innovations, which can eliminate identified gaps in IKEA Russia; to develop managerial recommendations of sustainable innovations for IKEA Russia with the detailed action plan for their realization ; to estimate the benefits according to the  Tripple  Bottom Line concept to evaluate the  risks of the proposed recommendations. In the contemporary world, the elaboration of the sustainable innovations becomes crucial in order to respond on the global environmental issues, creating the effective approaches in the way to gain the competitive advantage and to thrive in the fast-changing unpredictable environment. That is why the indicated theme is highly relevant in recent times. The current  qualification  paper will be performed in the forma t of the consulting project. It means, that  the results of the study will provide IKEA company with the practical managerial recommendations  for  the  introduction  of  the sustainable innovations . The proposals  will be  developed based  on the traits  of  international  sustainable development  strategy  of the company, the analysis of the external environment  and the   theoretical overview  connected to the sustainable development within the business .  The research methodology The study will be  conducted  on the basis of the analysis of  the  multiple sources, where the  primary sources of the information are  the  GSOM  guest lecture about sustainable development in IKEA  ( presented by IKEA sustainability coordinator, Elena  Karpova )  and  the   interviews  with the sustainable development manager s  of IKEA  in Russia,  Aleksandra  Nagornaya  and  Mba   Eyama  Rosa Pascual . The choice of the manager s  of sustainability department for the interview is connected to the fact, that the whole study is based on the evaluation and elaboration of sustainable initiatives within the company.  The manager s  of this exact department  are  responsible for the development and implementation of initiatives in sustainability field in Russian market; ther efore, it justifies the manager s ’  expertise.  The conducted interview was  structured since  I have prepared the questions before the meeting with interviewee. The secondary data sources of information involve research connected to the sustainable development field with the major emphasis on sustainable innovations. The sources of information have the high level of diversity, since they include both classic as well as the brand-new literature. The study includes information from the articles taken from the sources such as HBR management magazine, SCOPUS and Springer database, from the annual reports of the companies on sustainable development (IKEA) from the statistical databases (Statista, WCIOM). It is also based on the data taken from the consultancy reports on sustainability and innovations (Accenture,  Finexpertiza ), government data ( Rosstat ), business media (Vedomosti, rbc.ru) and the reports of the global organization (United Nations). Tools and instruments In the way  to achieve the stated goal s  through the identified  objectives, I will us e  the managerial instruments, which will help me to propose the  rational recommendations for the company. As IKEA has already introduced the sustainability strategy, I decided to evaluate how properly that strategy is reflected in the operational processes of the company. It is important to evaluate the operations in the way to identify whether the processes match with the sustainability strategic direction. To do so, I have chosen the instrument of the Value chain analysis proposed by Michael Porter. It is viable in the way to  consistent ly assess the  ini tiatives  within the  process  of value creation, analyzing   each aspect of the company’s activities for the compliance with sustainable development. In addition to the analysis of the internal processes, it is viable to develop the analysis of the external factors, which do affect or may affect the company’s performance in sustainability field. To do so the  PETSEL-analysis  (which was introduced by the Harvard professor F. Aguilar,) was chosen . The application of the PESTEL- analysis too l will be  addressed on the furniture retail industry in Russia. Thereby, the instrument will help to reveal the most relevant factors of the external environment for IKEA Russia and  consequently  find out, which  improvements  may become the most urgent and significant  one . The major value of PESTEL-analysis is that it gives an opportunity to assess the current situation within Russian market through the various perspectives, which are political, economic, social, technological,  environmental,  and legal.    Afterwards , I will present the s ustainability-oriented innovations framework , proposed by the Network for Business Sustainability.  The framework  is considerably viable, since it   will help to analyze the IKEA Russia performance in sustainable development area through the prism of sustainable innovations. The beneficial point of the model is that it indicates the extent of innovations’ sustainability in terms of the context of organizational development. Moreover, it gives the detailed description of each  innovation  types depending on the contexts. Therefore, the sustainability-oriented   innovations framework  may become the  universal  benchmarking  for  assessment of  IKEA R ussia innovations .   So that,  I believe  that it  will identify the precise gaps in IKEA Russia  sustainable innovations . Therefore, the identified gaps may  become the basis for the recommendations to implement. Following this, I am going to utilize the instrument, called SWOT-analysis, which will summarize the findings from the external and internal analyses. SWOT-analysis will be also dedicated to the sustainable development of the company. So that it will give an opportunity to evaluate how the external and internal factors influence the achievement of the company’s strategic vision, identifying key strengths, weaknesses,  opportunities,  and threats. I believe, that with the SWOT-analysis development, I will be able to make a conclusion, what IKEA needs to undertake in the way to solve the existing managerial problem. When the recommendations will be proposed, it will be essential to evaluate the ir  benefits, which they will bring for the company  and externalities .  So that the recommendations  will be evaluated through the metrics of the Triple Bottom Line concept, which unites the social,  environmental,  and eco nomic  aspects.  That analysis is viable, since the innovations will be proposed for the sustainable development of the  company, which  require s  the business to generate the economic profit along with the value in the social and environmental field s . Furthermore, the recommendations   have to  be assessed on  t he possible  risks, which  they may face with in Russian market.  To make the risk analysis I will use the instrument called the Risk Scenario Matrix. It is beneficial, since it helps to evaluate the risks according to two major aspects, which are the extent of the risk’s impact and the extent of risk’s likelihood.  The framework will be essential for the consulting project, since it will help to ensure that the recommendations are viable and  all risks of innovations are  considered .   CHAPTER 1.  SUST AINABILITY IN IKEA COMPANY Company description The brand IKEA was founded in Sweden in 19 43 by Ingvar Kamprad as the company  that distributes  household products, such as  watches and pens. However, in 1948 it  changed its direction and  started the development in the furniture industry. At that  time,  the goal of the CEO was to make the  highly qualitative  furnit ure available for many people. B ased on  the  economy of scale concept ,   t he company was selling  furnishing products   at  lower prices  through the catalogues, which helped to reach customers from remote areas .  Because of the most available prices in the market,   IKEA  brand became  increasingly  popular in Sweden and yet in 1958 the first  showroom  of IKEA was opened  ( Koshelnik , 2015) .  IKEA stores were expanding  more   around the world  a nd  by the end of 2020  there are  yet  445  IKEA stores operating worldwide , selling mainly the  ready-to-assemble  furniture, accessories for home design as well as  useful household appliances .   In fact, in 2013 there were just 345 stores, meaning that  for  7 years IKEA  increased its presence by 100 physical stores  around the world  (Statista, 2020). And so  far,  the goal of the company remains unchanged. Today it is reflected in the current global vision of IKEA that is “to create a better everyday life for the many people”   providing customers with various  sustainable and  hi gh-quality  home furnishing.  The vision of IKEA is also  aimed  to care about lives of co-workers  and   employees of their  suppliers.  In the long-term  perspective,  the  goal derived from  the  vision  of IKEA is  to  influence positively on the  entire world , promoting sustainable living for all IKEA communities  and stakeholders .   Under this  vision,  the company implements its business idea, offering the  furniture  and other products  at the  affordable prices , giving the chance for  all people  to  have  well- designed products for their houses  (IKEA ,  n.d.) .   In fact ,  this business approach works  successfully  in the stable economic conditions , since IKEA sales  were steadily growing  each year and by the  end of 2019,  they reached €41.3 billion  (IKEA,   n.d.) ,  while the profit amounted to €1.5 billion .  More than 211 000 of co - workers  were  hired by IKEA at 2019 (IKEA, n.d.) . IKEA is expanding even in the crisis period of the pandemic, since t he number of stores  was  increasing   during  2020 . How ever ,  due to the worldwide lockdowns  a t  quarantine period the average amount of all IKEA stores ’   visitors  decreased from 1,000  mln . in 2019  to 825  mln .  people  in 2020 . Nevertheless,  the sales  of the compan y dropped  insignificantly since   IKEA  is considerably flexible to  adapt under the economic challenges. During 2020 the  e-commerce market was the key to sustain IKEA sales.   The sales accounted for  € 39. 6  bln ., so that it was the first year with such a sales decline over the  extended period  of IKEA history.  The share of online sales enlarged by 10%   b y the end of 2020 , achieving 18%.   During 2020 IK EA.com has been visited 4  bln . t imes worldwide, meaning tha t it is 1  bln . m ore, comparing to the previous 2019 year  (Statista, n.d.) . In addition to the sales of furnishing products,  the company is  operating in the  other integrated in IKEA stores businesses, that are cafes and restaurants selling Swedish traditional food.  Moreover , based on the popularity of IKEA cafes, the groceries stor es were also developed under IKEA  brand name. Due to the globalization trends , each year  the company is increasing its scope under the beneficial strategy of low price along with the high quality of goods.   Moreover, in the  fast-changing  environment,  the  company is constantly working on  the continuous improvement of  all  business  units . It is developing innovations in production, elaborating  novel items  for  a  brand in the way to meet customers’ needs , improving its operations toward sustainability . IKEA continues to work on the business expansion  in the way  to reach as many people as possible.  Each year IKEA introduce s  the enhanced innovative products for the customers.  The  prototypes  of the innovat ions  are  being shown to the stakeholders  each year  during the  IKEA press-event called  Democratic Design Days. The innovative products  are  illustrated not only in terms of the uniqueness of  the usage,  art and design , its functionality , but also in terms of the ir  sustainable production and eco-friendly utilization  and recycling .   In fact, during 2019 the company introduced mo re than 2000 new products  (Inter IKEA Group | Newsroom,  2019) . Sustainability as an underlying concept In recent years, the concept of sustainable development becomes more disputed within the society. Obviously, the sustainability area is developing due to the growing awareness among people  and communities  in regards of the  severe  societal challenges and  environmental issues , such as global warming, deforestation, waste disposal, biodiversity loss, etc . Despite of the fact that Sustainable Development term was mentioned in 1969 by International Union for Conservation of Nature  ( Acciona , n.d.) ,  there  is  still not a universal definition ,  which is able to cover  various  sustainability  dimensions, such as environmental protection, conservation of resources, habitat preserv ation, biodiversity,  the production of  recyclable and  pollution-free products, sustainable operations  within the  value creation ,  living conditions  enhancement   among  vulnerable populations,  fair working conditions and  etc  ( D’heur , 2015) .   Furthermore, the  sustainability concept  is still developing and expanding. However, there is a stable definition for that term covering common sense, which is used broadly among all over the world:  “ S ustainable Development  is the development that meets the needs of the present without  compromising the ability of the future gene rations to meet their own needs ”  (World Commission on Environment and Development, 1987).   That term   has been described  in 1987 in the report “Our Common Future” proposed by United Nations Brundt lan d Commission.  The Commission has identified the terrifying effects on the environment and climate change caused by the  comprehensive globalization as well as  rapid  population increase and  economic growth. Basing on the thorough research that was made with the help of the contributions from various v oluntary  stakeholders, the  practical solutions  for the global  challenges'  e limination were elaborated. “Our  Common Future ”  program has represented the long-term strategies and practical  tactics  for developed and developing countries that assisted in dealing with the environmental problems. It was aimed to reach the sustainability by 2000 and beyond. Owing to that program ,  the world had eliminated many ecological issues. However, due to the  fast-growing  economy, the overpopulation and consequently the  important level  of consumption, the  number  of goals and tactics for the sustainability  have  increase d  even more .  So that, t he United Nations Members States was  regularly  establish ing updated agendas for improving the health and education, proclaiming equality and stimulating the economic growth, while sacrificing the planet resources  (United Nations, n.d.) . IKEA  Lagom  community In fact , IKEA was operating under sustainability principles starting from its foundation.  At its  beginning,  the company was developing products for initial group of IKEA customers, who were primarily Swedes, the majority of whom are living based on the  Lagom  philosophy. The concept of  Lagom  indicates, that the “the right amou nt is  the  best”.  Therefore,  the business of  IKEA company was rising under that philosophy, meaning that it utilized  exactly right  amount of all resources needed for the production, including materials, water, electricity  and etc.   For instance, yet in 1953 the company  produced  the idea of the flatpacks and self-assembly of t he furniture  that rationalized the space in trailers, diminishing the number of shipments, and therefore, minimizing the carbon emissions  (IKEA, 2011) . Thus, since the beginning of 1950s IKEA brand continues to cultivate this philosophy, utilizing resources more consciously. What is more, through  Lagom  philosophy the company motivates all stakeholders around the world to utilize the products in such a way that will sacrifice planet resources,  producing more sustainable products and providing customers and suppliers  with various useful  advice  in regards of the question: “How to live in a more responsible way?”  Nowadays,  the company  is still operating under this philosophy, actively promoting a unique  program “Live  Lagom ” that  is aimed to   inspire and  u nite more people  in the communities  worldwide to care about the planet, by giving the second life for utilized goods  (IKEA, n.d.) . History of sustainability  in IKEA The company was expanding  Lagom  philosophy, continuing to illustrate the active  participation ,  performing globally in various areas . In regards of the societal efforts, IKEA founded a charitable fund, IKEA Foundation, to become the considerable part in philanthropic incentives in 1982.  It  broadly  supports UNICEF and  “ Save Children ” programs to protect children’s’  rights in Pakistan and India, as the countries of the cotton growing communities. Furthermore, IKEA updated all suppliers’ contracts , so that they function according to  International Labor Organization (ILO) standards on child labor. And in 2000, the supplier code of conduct, IWAY, that includes the environmental and societal standards  for all IKEA suppliers , has been introduced. Considering the environmental incentives, during 1990-s the company has published the first Environmental Policy to decrease any possible damage on the environment, employed the forestry manager to develop more responsible practices and established a Waste Management Manual. Based on these successful innovations for sustainability and their impacts, IKEA published the first Social and Environmental Responsibility Report  yet  in 2004.  Nevertheless , the company did not stop at this point. In 2006 it initiated the ambition to run all operati ons from 100% renewable energy. In fact, yet in 2010  IKEA   proposed the  long-term plan called  Sustainability Direction for 2015 with the environmental and societal   objectives and tactics in five main dimensions, that are sustainable production, creation of low carbon society, waste disposal, efficient water utilization and social responsibility  (IKEA, 2011) . However, f rom its foundation the company wa s developing under unsustainable business  model since  the major operations were functioning under  linear economy principles. IKEA did not have the conscious approach in its production. The company did not utilize any waste and did not recycle the used materials and products. Nevertheless, according to sustainability reports,  nowadays  it is steadily turning to more sustainable direction  in the whole production process , implementing  increased   initiatives from  the  circular economy.   IKEA company as a global  issues'  defender   Importantly, in 2015  United Nations Brundt lan d  Commission  introduce d  the  2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development to illustrate essential problems, supported by the terrific facts and statistics and ways to overcome the global issues. It includes 17 essential goals for sustainability that motivate the peace and prosperity for planet and people  (United Nations, n.d.) .  It mostly concerns about three key parts: nature and environment, social  development,  and economic growth. According to United Nations, during next 15 years the goals will  promote environmental and societal responsibility and  stimulate regular and universal actions in the following areas of crucial significance. It was emphasized by United Nations  that the goals were a pproved by all Governments  with the involvement of Parliaments, private sectors, international institutions, local  authorities,  and civil society. It was claimed that the high level of effectiveness towards goals achievement  would  be reached through the colla boration and partnership of that  institutions, which can join their active efforts to change the world. So that, all the governments  must  “… call on all businesses to apply their creativity and innovation to solving sustainable development  challenges. ”  ( United N ations Global Compact and KPMG,  2017) .   Following this,  nowadays, there is a tendency that many companies around the world change their activities to make them coincidental with sustainable development. It means that businesses concern not only  about  the fact of   being profitable, but also  about  global issues, taking care of which can enhance the quality of life for the society worldwide. Sustainable development as the strategic  direction  is aimed to establish persistent existence of company’s stakeholders by reacting on the current global challenges and evolving them for future steady enhancement. Each year United Nations publishes the reports, where the global significant achievement for the  entire year  is described. One of the companies that actively works on the eliminating the global challenges through 17 goals by innovative approach is IKEA company.   The sustainability incentives within IKEA are considered now as the integrated part of the core business and correlates with the mission of the company. In addition to this, the shared value concept is in the hurt of its strategy. It means that the business includes all direct and indirect stakeholders in the product creation processes and operational value chain  ( D’heur , 2015) .  According to the presentation from company representative, IKEA is constantly developing under the openness  to the mutual collaboration with all stakeholders, generating the value-add from the partnership both for the company and its   associate. For instance, there is a strategic partnership of IKEA and WWT aimed at the gas emissions reduction in IKEA functioning  (IKEA, 2011) . People & Planet Positive, the IKEA sustainability strategy Since the company was following the  Lagom  philosophy from its foundation, it was decided to expand that concept to innovative sustainable development program “People and Planet Positive” and integrate it in the global strategy of IKEA in 2012. According to the interview with the sustainable  development manager of IKEA,  at that time the  program included three main directions, which are described in the Table 1: Table  1 .  People and Planet Positive. 2012 A more sustainable life at home It is aimed to develop and promote the products and solutions for more sustainable life of customers,  suppliers,  and co-workers. Resource and energy independence It is based on the  resource's  utilization within the limits of the planet (renewable energy, energy efficiency, low carbon transportation). A better life for people and communities It covers social responsibility initiatives  to contribute to a better life for people and communities impacted by IKEA business. Source: adapted from IKEA Group   (20 12). Sustainability Report FY12  However,  because  IKEA company operates in  an extremely sensitive   industry (its major products are furniture made from the wooden materials) and the global challenges in regards of the ecological and social issues along with all  stakeholder s ’  demands  require the company  to become more responsible, the global  strategy of IKEA was modified.   According  t o the statistics, IKEA company by 2013 used  about 1%  of the wor ld's commercial wood supply  (GIZMODO, 2013)  and today the figure is even higher . So  that , in 2018 it launched the updated sustainability strategy based on the Sustainable Development Goals and the global mission of the company that is to improve everyday life of people. The goals presented in “People and Planet Positive”  strategy   is  aspired to be developed till 2030. The strategy also covers three areas in the sustainability ,  which are described in the Table 2: Table 2. People & Planet Positive  According to the  sustainability report from 2020 , the company has achieved the great sustainable development results.   Nowadays, the primary strategic direction of IKEA company is to move from the idea of mass consumption to mass circulation of the  products.  In fact ,  currently the company  sorts about 75% of its waste, giv ing  the second life  for the products and packages,  us ing  recycled materials in   manufacturing  in all countries, where it is operating . Moreover, IKEA  designs,  produces,  and  distributes the  unique recyclable products for home, such as LED-lamps, dishwashers, taps with aerators, etc. These products  are  developed   not only for life convenience, but also for  saving water and energy  during the  consumption  and utilization by  IKEA customers. Furthermore, all of the cotton and 91% of the wood are used by IKEA from more sustainable sources  (IKEA, 2019) . The   power, which boosts businesses toward  the  modification and introduction of innovati ve approaches, is formed by the   global mega trends  that are increasing their  scope and influence on the organizations .  T he research  called  “Megatre nds and Sus tainable development” introduced  by  Prandecki  et al.,  illustrate s   those   meg a trends   that  nowadays  affects the sustainable development orientation within the companies and  that  will continue to influence it in the nearest future . The authors of the research identify megatrends as “the global forces affecting the state, the market, and the society, acting many years in advance”  ( Prandecki  et al., 2013) .  The investigation has indicated the following megatrends as the most significant in terms of their influence on sustainability: U rbanization .  The approximate prediction for 2050 claims that more than 2/3 of the world populatio n will live in the urban areas  (Ritchie and  Roser , 2018) .   In fact,  urbanization leads to  changing the consumption habits, meaning that people in big cities tend to increa se their level of consumption. Consequently, this  t rend  influences on the accelerating utilization of  scarce and non-renewable resources. Moreover, the  living in the urban areas implies  the enlargement of cities,  and  therefore,  affect s the increase of CO 2  emissions, which negatively affects the climate change and health of world’s inhabitants. The increase of a ir transport.  The globalization trends boosted the increase of the airplanes number both for passengers’ and for cargo transportation. During 15 years from 2004 till the pre-COVID 2020 period the plane traffic almost doubled , achieving 40,3  mln . flights per year  (Statista, 2020) .  The aviation industry produces the incredible emissions (12% of all transportation emissions), which  also  affects  significantly on  the climate change  (David Suzuki Foundation, n.d.) .  However, in would be better to identify the globalization itself as a megatrend that influence sustainability, since  the  overall effect from the globalization is much higher than just from air traffic. The globalization boosts the international business, cross-border trade and travels that cumulatively increases the worldwide transportation, which is not environmentally friendly. The network society.  The development of modern technologies gives an opportunity for networking among people  all over the world. The availability of big flow of  free  information  increase s  the consciousness toward the environmental and societal challenges global ly among individuals and groups, since consumers may easily join in  team s and communicate easily , expressing their dissatisfaction  about undesirable business practices.  However, I would call the technological  development itself as a megatrend, since it changes not only the way how people engage each other into sustainability pr oblems, but also the way how business   modifies its strate gies, products and value chains to develop under sustainability challenges. The growing pressure on the environment.  Three main aspects, which are the climate change, the inefficient use of limited and scarce  resources and biodiversity loss  are considered as the most essential ones in terms of environmental challenges. There is a trend that civil society increase s  its consciousness toward sustainability issues , which are  caused by the broad expansion of the globalization and urbanization. These factors influence the growth of the higher environmental footprint such as the CO 2  emissions, soil degradation, deforestation, waste generation, etc., which cau se the threatening  societal problems, especially for the next generation s . Moreover, there is a significant growth i n number of population (about 26 % rise in 20 20 , comparing with the figure in 2000)  ( Worldometer , n.d.).  It  results in the growth of the waste due to the increasing consumption. So nowadays, the world population is using 50% more resources than what planet is able to regenerate. In fact, the historical data even illustrates that each 1% growth in GDP leads on average to 0 .4 % increase in resources utilization  (Accenture, 2014) .   That fact illustrates that  the raising GDP is not always considered as a main wealth of the country  (Global Footprint Network, 2012) .   Another research developed by  Lasz lo  (2008)   claims  that  currently  under the global mega trends   the business is developing  in  changing competitive environment . These mega trends affect  the companies  considerabl y,  motivating  the organizations  to shift  their  traditional  business models from creation on ly the shareholders’ value. Nowadays,  enterprises tend  to establish  the  business models under the  synergy developing  the social, economic and environmental values  simultaneously .   According to the researcher, the  trends  indicated below  changes the business  landscape : The considerable gap between social needs and values created by the public affairs.  The following situation  happens  due to the fact that  government and NGOs are not able to fight effectively with the sustainable challenges alone. Therefore, the business may be engaged in the value creation that will be aimed to minimization of environmental issues and enlargement of values for covering demanding social needs, considering the problems  as opportunities . Society expects the increasing responsibility from the business.  Nowadays, the conscious attention of consumers toward organization s  request s  not only the minimization of the emissions, but also the  modification  of entire business models and value chains,  that will be focused on the creation of  only sustainable products and services. The technological advancement and the collaborative platforms.  The following trend is the  similar to  the “Network society trend” and claims that organizations nowadays are mor e dependent on  the consumers and their loyalty, since any inappropriate business decision may be banned by the consumers all over the world . Change of market mechanism.  It is stated that the social and environmental initiatives add the financial value for the business, since it becomes more attractive for the “green” investors. It means that the development of more sustainable practices leads businesses to generate higher sales from the eco-friendly consumers and lower costs from circular economy implementation. New legislation.  The governments develop new laws, restrictions and penalties for the business in the way to protect the environment.  By doing so, they initiate the transition to a new way of doing business that radically improves resource productivity  and environmental footprint.    However, there are still not  single g lobal environmental norms, since the  legislation  regarding sustainability varies from country to country, meaning that in some regions the organizations are still no t   punished for damaging the environment  (Laszlo, 2008) . To sum up, the external environment and changing consumers’ demand motivate companies to transform and  to  move away from the developing within obsolete linear model toward the innovative sustainability business models . It is in significant importance, since  the  companies will tend to receive  greater advantages of new opportunities from the transformation, implementing effective solutions, which are aimed at solving the substantial issues in terms of the economic,  ecological  and societal dimension s . It is believed that the transition toward mo re sustainable approach will provide  the companies with the greater benefits, especially in the long-run perspective.   2.2  S ustainability-based analysis of the external environment with the application to  the  furniture industry After the evaluation of the glo bal mega trends, it is essential to analyze  precisely , what impact these megatrends had on business and how it  influence s  on  sustainable development in Russian market, since the consulting project is applied for the company that is operat ing in Russia. The shift toward the remote work thanks to the availability of technologies.  The pandemic changes the composition of the labor market, since the traditional offices closed and therefore, companies proclaimed the distant work. Homes became the area where people spent their whole days, so the home utilities started to play the crucial role for the households. Due to the trend of distant work and education, people tend to purchase more furnishing facilities to make home areas convenient for work, which is positively affects the furnishing industry.  However, there is a negative aspect as well, since the remote work boosted the employees’ unwillingness to go back to work in offices, so that many companies proclaim the hybrid (online and offline) types of working. In future, it may lead to the fact that more companies will decide to work only remotely, so that it can affect the closure of offices and, therefore, influence on the decrease of the demand for office furniture. The increasing popularity of e-commerce.  The pandemic lead s  to significant changes in the retail industry. The statistics illustrates that overall online shopping has increased by 45% in Russia, and Russian citizens made about 800  mln . orders during 2020, which is 65% higher than in the previous year  (New retail.ru, 2020) .  The growth of e-commerce is the great opportunity for furnishing companies to develop online sales in a  better way . Companies may enhance the customers’ experience of online shopping, proposing new  online  services, based on the latest disruptive technologies. Therefore, in future there may be the tendency of  the  decreasing number of physical stores. The online shopping may be also considered as a n   environmentally friendly  aspect, since the  proper logistics of companies’ deliveries harm the ecology with emissions  to the lesser extent comparing to the  households’  car travels  for  shopping in malls. Envi ronmental factors: H igh level of waste  production .  N owadays, despite of the fact that in 2017 Russian environmental safety  strategy was issued, there is still poor management of the waste generated  by both households and businesses . Moreover, in 2019 the enterprises have generated  above 7. 8  billion   tons of  the industrial waste. The amount is growing each year, and to illustrate the speedy growth of waste production, in 2002 the  indicated  figure was just 2 billion tons  ( Finexpertiza , 2020) .   Considering the waste generated by households, there is no tradition for the waste separation and recycling  in Russia . The major waste is moved to the increasing landfills, which cause the considerable environmental challenges and  therefore,   influence on  the health and well-being of society. Statistics illustrates that more than 90% of waste in the country is brought in the landfills. To prevent the ecological catastrophe,  in 2018  government has introduced  the National  Project “Ecology”  which is  aimed to protect the environment.  Solid waste management system for household and industrial waste was included in the agenda of the Project  (Netherlands Embassy in the Russian Federation, 2020) .  However, in 2021 there is still poor infrastructure developed by government for waste separation and recycling.  Thus, the  businesses can take the lead  to struggle with waste challenges of households. Increasing a ir and water pollution .  The air and water pollution influences considerably on climate change, biodiversity loss and makes the negative impact on the societal health.   Even though there are many technologies, which are aimed to the minimization of emissions of the production, air pollution is  still  increasing.   The number of cases of hig h and extreme air pollution is growing . The research of consulting company  Finexpertiza  (2020)  stated that in the first quarter of 2020 the number of cases of high air pollution achieved the record during last 5 year (44 cases). Considering the water pollution,  it  is also a big issue. Nevertheless,  starting from 2015 the number of high and extreme water pollution cases were decreasing, achieving 2 , 713, in 2019 it has grown  again and achieved  3 , 132  ( Finexpertiza , 2020) .   2020  illustrated the drop  of high-water pollution , however, there were  still  extreme cases such as water pollution in Kamchatka and Norilsk.  The high level of pollution  influences  the climate change. In fact, in Russia it is getting warmer 2.5 times faster than average in  the world  (Vedomosti, 2020) .  Thus, the unfavorable ecological situation influences all industries to pay more attention toward environment. Deforestation  and forests fires .  As it was illustrated in the  Rosstat  statistics (in the  Figure above ) , Russia considerably suffers from the deforestation due to the huge amount of burnt forests lands.  D uring 2020 there were more  than  6 ,000 forest fires that covered  and damaged more than 990 ,000 hectares of the forest  (Made for minds, n.d.) .  Only  for  5 months of 2020 the forest area decreased by 26%  due to the fires .  However,  there is still an existing law that permits not to extinguish the fires in the hard-to-reach areas  (called “control zones”) , since it can be not economically efficient for regions. Fortunately, the law is under the consideration of Ministry of Nature that want s to diminish the “control zones” areas from 50% of forest fund to 5%  (Rbc.ru, 2020) .  Another problem connected to the timber industry is an illegal logging,   however statistics illustrates that the number of illegal logging diminished by 8% in 2020  (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment of the Russian Federation , 2021) .  Nevertheless, the problem connected to the deforestation is still essential, since afforestation makes still insignificant effect comparing to high-level of deforestation.  So that it may affect the furnishing industry, since the scarcity of  the resources may influence on  the  resources price, increasing the prices of final products. Legal factors The p rotection of human rights .   As sustainable development proclaims the societal equality, it is essentially to mention that there is a law in Russian legislation that obliges companies to develop the business, providing employees with the safe and fair working conditions. All standards that are needed to follow by employees are stated in the Labor Code of Russian Federation. T he Code is developed in the way  to protect employees from the discrimination regarding labor rights and freedoms. It describes the detailed working conditions that employer is obliged to provide to employees. Moreover, the attention is also given to the dangerous labor conditions, which influence the health of employees. As furnishing companies are developing with the professional efforts of their  employees, companies have to comply with the standards indicated in the Labor Code, treating all employees equally  (Consultant Plus, n.d.) . Legislation aimed to protect  the forests .  As furnishing industry is directly con nected to the wood extraction, Russian  legislation toward the forestry is considered.  Forests cover about 45% of the territory of the country and Russia is the first in the ranking of possessing the biggest amount of timber resources (1/5 of world’s timber reserves are located in Russia). Therefore, timber industry is under the strict governmental supervision  ( Priroda   Rossii , n.d.) .   The Forest Code is the key forestry low. It was introduced to  protect the natural resources from illegal logging, to set up the rules for the wood extraction, to develop initiatives for afforestation and conservation and to preserve forests from the regular fires  (Forest Legality Initiative, n.d.) .  Nevertheless , there is a negative point in the Code, which let s  regions not to extinguish fires in the “control zones” due to the inaccessibility of these fores ts and consequently high municipal expenditures for their extinguishing .   However,  in reality forests are highly  important  for  the  climate change prevention. In addition to this, the research developed by  consulting company  BCG claims that cumulative forests value  in Russia  exceeds the value of oil by 28%  (BCG, 2020) .    So that, furnishing companies must not only comply with the rules identifi ed in the Forest Code, but also  be  more  proactive toward the afforestation maintenance. Poor stimulation of  “green initiatives”.  The international research developed by Vivid Economics  (2021)  has analyzed the  decrees about the  governmental support toward the stimulation of industries, which were harmed by the pandemic crisis. It was revealed that about  $ 11.8  trillions  were allocated as an anti-crisis support worldwide.  The interesting fact is that in Russia all subsidies were introduced to support  only those industries, which  negatively  affect  the environment in comparison with other countries that stimulated the  businesses based on  “green ”  initiatives. Actually, that  situation  may  cause the   negative perception of the business toward the development of sustainability programs,  since  the implementation of sustainability practices  can be evaluated irrationally  by  the  enterprises  due to the lack of  the   encouragement and  support from the governmental authorities. However,  due to the lockdowns  the enhancement  of comfortable conditions  at home became the priority for citizens. The pandemic changed the working conditions, and perception of the business about the necessity of offices. Nevertheless, despite the economic recession and the  introduction of  Russian environmental safety strategy, the government does not effectively stimulate the “green” initiatives .  Considering environmental factors, Russia has the high level of  waste production,  air and water pollution  and deforestation  that influ ence the climate change. So that, to deal with sustainability challenges and changing societal demands, the business needs to introduce and expand the  sustainable  innovations. 2.3 The  analysis of the sustainable practices of IKEA through the prism of Value Chain The  evaluation of the core drivers for sustainability and the  analysis of the external  factors, influencing IKEA  Russia , have   justified  the need for companies to implement the  broad range of  sustainable practices in the way to meet the growing challenges.  So that, it is necessary to evaluate the  current  sustainable initiatives of IKEA Russia   through the prism of the  theoretical frameworks  in the way to  fi nd the precise drawbacks  in a company, that does not comply with the megatrends and external environment .  There are many frameworks that analyzes the sustainability of the business, such as: Sustainable Value Framework proposed by S. Hart and M. Milstein, which evaluates the sustainable strategies of the company in terms of their orientations (external or internal) and in terms of their ambitions (prevention of todays or future issues). However, it focuses just on the strategic orientation of the business and does not provide the deep evaluation of the internal processes. Sustainable business model archetypes developed by  N.M.P.  Bocken  et al.  that  analyzes  the sustainable business models  in terms of technological, social and organizational perspectives. Even though t he framework is quite extended,  and evaluates the business model from various perspectives, it still does not provide the clear understanding, at which stages the business introduces the sustainable solutions. Value chain analysis  for  sustainability  (adapted from the Value Chain analysis developed by M. Porter) that evaluates how the internal processes of the company correlates with the sustainable principles.  The model gives the detailed deep   comprehension, how each operational division introduces the sustainable practices and indicates what else is needed to implement. So that, the following internal analysis of IKEA Russia will be based on the evaluation of the company in terms of the value chain, presented below. The value chain will be evaluated in the extended  perspective, since  it will also include the evaluation of the post-purchase utilization of the product by the customers. It is essential, since nowadays companies are responsible not only for the production and sales of the products, but also for its usage,  reusability  and recyclability. IKEA Russia Value Chain Source: adapted from Porter (1985) The primary activities of value chain  of the IKEA Russia   business  include  the design, materials and sourcing, production  itself , logistics, IKEA retail, distribution to consumers, usage of products  and  also  the  post-usage of products.   The process begins with the product  design , when  the models for the products are created . Following this, the company is working on the choice of needed  materials  to use in the production, finding the trustworthy  suppliers  to receive the materials . The next goes the production itself on the factories of IKEA. Afterwards there is a logistics  part  that helps IKEA to proceed the delivery  of products  from the production to  IKEA retail (or distribution centers) , which is followed by the distribution to the customer (with the delivery service). As the companies become more responsible toward consumption and post-consumption period, I have included usage of products and the post-usage of products in the value chain. The post-usage illustrates how the consumer is able to recycle the utilized product. Design.  The company  introduces  its sustainability initiatives starting from the products’   design . N owadays more than 4220 products are designed from the recyclable and renewable materials.   And by 2030 IKEA plans to make all the products range among all over the world   from renewable or recycled materials.  In addition to this, it is developing the products, which can be used in a sustainable manner (such as led lamps that economize the electricity usage).  The company unites the high quality, democratic design, the comfortable forms and low price in the design of all IKEA products.   IKEA Russia has got rid of all single-use plastic products, proposing more eco-friendly alternatives (IKEA, 2020) (IKEA, 2020). Materials and sourcing.   IKEA Russia  rents the forestry, whic h provides the company 20% of ra w materials for the production in Russia.  The company cares about the future of forests, so that it plants seedlings (about 20  mln . in 2019). The o ther 80% of the resources are purchased from the local suppliers. IKEA Russia carefully manages the forest resources extraction. The company employs the forest resources managers that verify the compliance of all IKEA suppliers with the standards indica ted in the universal IKEA IWAY C ode  of Conduct . The great emphasis is given to the checks of the wood extraction legitimacy  ( LesPromInform , 2017) .   In addition to this,  the majority of materials used in production are certified (FSC certification for wood materials, BCI certification for cotton materials, ASC for salmon, MSC for shrimps and UTZ for coffee and chocolate production).   Nevertheless, IKEA still uses leather, wool  and  feathers for its products . Even though IKEA introduces the Animal Welfare program, it still generates the issues, since animal agriculture considerably influences the deforestation and therefore increases the pollution  (Change.org, n.d.) . Production .  There are 4 factories located in Russia, which manufactures the products for IKEA stores.  Their production includes the stages from   wood   processing to the furniture production.  The company broadly implements the circularity principles within the  factories, since  all remaining chips from the manufacturing are used as a  resource  for the furniture or as a bioenergy. Nevertheless, there is an area for the improvemen t, since the energy, used in all  production  facilities  is not renewable  (only the factory in Novgorod is working on totally renewable energy) . In addition to 4 factories producing the wooden products, the company has introduced the production of the bed linen with the collaboration of the social enterprise Anton tut  Ryadom  (IKEA, 2020).   Nevertheless, the majority of the non-wooden materials are still imported from the  foreign markets. Purchasing and Logistics.  IKEA proposes the wide range of products, which are produced in 54 countries. It means that the long distances between the distribution centers among the countries produces the considerable number of emissions that negatively affects the ecological situation. As it was mentioned above, the globalization trend as well as the increasing amount of transportation damage the ecology in a significant manner. In addition to the unsustainability of the imported goods, internally in Russia the logistics also affects the environment. IKEA company has the distribution center in Moscow region that became the intermediary between the Russian production and IKEA stores and international production and Russian stores. However, it would be more efficient if the company was able to send the products to the stores and even to homes of the customers straight from the storages nearby factories, avoiding unnecessary path routes to distribution center. So that, it would give an opportunity for IKEA Russia to minimize the environmental footprint of CO2 emissions. IKEA  Marketing & Sales .  IKEA stores are designed in such a way that inspire the purchases of the sustainability-based products. For instance, there  are  banners nearby some products that  highlight  the sustainability  and recyclability of the materials. In addition to his, IKEA Russia introduces the banners which emphasizes, how IKEA products are minimizing the resources needed for the product use. Moreover, it motivates customers to purchase the separate containers, which make the waste separation process available for all households.  In addition to this, it promotes the water bottles to minimize the plastic usage and food containers to eliminate the food throwing.   IKEA Russia introduces the energy efficient lighting in their stores  and also  implements the practices that minimizes the water usage in the store s  and in IKEA Restaurants.  IKEA company successfully introduces the renewable energy in its stores, establishing the solar panels and solar collectors.  However,  there is  st ill no totally renewable energy, which  is able to  provide all  stores   around Russia  with  fully  sustainable energy. Even though the company established the solar panels in some of the stores, the major electricity is still non-renewable  (IKEA, 2020) .   According to the internal company’s review, nowadays  Russia is considered as the most challenging market for the switching to renewable energy . Considering the waste recycling IKEA stores nowadays recycle 72% of waste. It sorts more than 20 types of the waste (IKEA, 2020).  However, there ar e  challenges with other types of waste recycling, since th e company faces issues, such as the legislation in regards of the confirmation of waste type, the waste gathering, keeping and its transference, the choice of the contractor for recycling. Distribution to consumers .  IKEA Russia has an ambition to distribute all products by the electric vehicles in the way to minimize the emission  and also   to  decrease the costs of the fuel usage.  During 2020, IKEA introduced 3 electric vehicles for the delivery, decreasing the amount of CO2 emissions by 5 tones. In 2021 Saint Petersburg has also released the project with electric vehicles for delivery.  However, in Russian market it is significantly hard to achieve  its ambition  on   a large scale , since there are few   charging stations for vehicles among the cities of IKEA’s presence. Usage of products.  Within the circular economy direction IKEA Russia has introduced the innovative products, that minimize the energy utilization of the household. For instance, there are many energies and water efficient appliances, such as LED lamps, rechargeable batteries and lights, dishwashers, kitchen and bathroom taps, etc. In addition to this, through the Better Living App the company tells the consumers about the sustainability habits, inspires the customers to purchase the spare parts for the broken furniture or to use the IKEA repairing service ( YouDo ), rather than purchase the new one. One of the aims of IKEA Russia is to increase the sales of the sustainable goods even more, nowadays it accounts for 39.8%. Post-usage of products .  IKEA introduced the service of the furniture buyout that gives an opportunity for customers to sell the utilized products of IKEA back to the store, so that IKEA will be able to resell the second-hand furniture at the discounted price.  In addition to this, customers are able to sell their products directly to each other in the collaborative platform introduced by IKEA. The company accepts the utilized products, such as old furniture, bed linen, lamps to recycle it into the raw materials.  The products with the goods quality  are  sent to the charitable fund  Vtoroe   Dyhanie .  However, IKEA Russia still is not able to give the “second life” for all types of products that it sells. Moving on to the supporting activities within the Value chain, it includes the Firm Infrastructure, Human Resource management, Technology Development and Procurement. Firm Infrastructure .  As “People & Planet Positive”  IKEA  strategy  is based on the sustainable development, each year the company releases the sustainability report, illustrating its performance in all three areas of the strategy.  Thus, the sustainable development department plays the crucial role for the multinational strategy commitment.   The company  is working hard to collaborate  not only the suppliers  and contractors  into its sustainability projects, but also the charitable funds (such as  Perspektiva ,  Detskie   Derevni ,  MasterOK ) and companies from other industries (such as  MVideo  and  Sportmaster ), increasing the engagement for broadening sustainable initiatives. Human Resource management.  According to Sustainable Development reports of IKEA company  promotes the diversity and inclusion for the employment. It  in spires suppliers to employ the most vulnerable people. In Russia, the company has collaborated with the   charity fund “Raul” that is assisting the young people with disabilities and graduates from orphanages with the employment.  Nowadays, IKEA Russia employs 1 0 disabled people, who are working in  IKEA store, distribution center and IKEA factory . In addition to this, the company broadly communicates the sustainability practices among employees to inspire them to be  more conscious about sustainability challenges. Sales managers are trained in such a way that they  are able to  motivate customers to purchase environmental-friendly products.  Technology Development .  IKEA Russia has implemented the modern technologies that enable to produce the heating and energy from the biomaterials  (IKEA, n.d.) .  Nevertheless, there is a huge space for improvement, since not all production facilities and stores in  Russia are highly sustainable. IKEA Russia may introduce the disruptive technologies, which will minimize the emissions, which will eliminate the waste generation in the production. In addition to this, the detailed data analytics may enhance the logistics, minimizing the costs and emissions.  Procurement .  The procurement stage illustrates the relations with the supplie rs. IKEA has released the IWAY Code of Co nduct that established the requirement for the suppliers, including the  demands for the materials extraction, for the distribution, for the minimization of the environmental footprint  and also  for development of fair labor conditions. IKEA managers constantly verifies the compliance of the suppliers  and also  contractors  with the standards identified in IWAY   by the auditors .  If the inaccuracies are revealed, IKEA gives the company the period to eliminate issues and then check the supplier again. Nevertheless, there are meaningful drawbacks in Russia that are needed to be eliminated. It is nowadays in the great importance, since the challenges from the external environment requires the business not only to be economic-focused, but also to become responsible in all aspects of the societal and environmental dimensions. Moreover, the company has the urgency to commit to the People & Planet Positive objectives by 2030. 2.4  Th e transition from the traditional innovation toward sustainability-oriented innovation Based on the analysis above, it can be concluded that nowadays the changing external environment with  the  urgent issues regarding sustainability considerably affects the business. In the way to survive and prosper, the companies are in need to  adopt sustainable innovations, since sustainability-oriented innovations is nowadays considered as a competitive advantage for companies.  It is really significant tactics, since according to C.K.  Prahaland , the elaboration of the innovative “next practices” provides the companies with the “mega-opportunities”  (Prahalad, 2010) . The follo wing part  of the project  is aimed to identify what the  sustainable innovations   are , which value they provide for the organizations,  what are  the  similarity  between the traditional  innovations and sustainability-o riented innovations and how the companies with the successful sustainable innovations may enhance their value s. The answers to these questions will be provided based on the thorough research called “Innovating for sustainability” developed by authors of the Network for Business Sustainability  (Network for Business Sustainability, 2012) . However, f irstly, the traditional innovations will be analyzed.  The term “innovation” indicates “introduction of something new”  (Purcell, 2019) .  McKinsey states that the innovations in business are “consistent streams of new products, services, processes, and business models”, which are introduced in the way to sustain the growth of the company.  The innovation may be  implemente d as the development of new products, processes, business models  and also  as the enhancement of the existing ones . (McKinsey Quarterly) .  Now adays, for almost 2/3 of all companies  the  innovation s   are  the top-three management priority.  Innovations support  companies in  the process of differentiation  in the highly competitive environment.  As a matter of fact , the success of  the  innovation implementation will have higher probability i n case when the company  is able to investigate outside the boundaries of the company  ( Ringel  et. al., 2020) .  Nevertheless , there is no optimal innovation that can be applicable for all companies  from the same  industry since   the external context plays the significant role  for its implementation . In addition to this, the individual business context is also essential for choosing  and developing  the innovation.   BCG have identified 5 key business innovation types, that are: business model innovation, internal processes innovation , go-to-market innovation, new service innovation and new product innovation. In addition to this, the key innovation enablers  that accelerate the innovation within the business  were indicated: advanced analytics, technology platforms, digital  design,  and mobile capabilities  (Manly et. al., 2017) .  The figure below illustrates the top innovation  priorities with their enablers distributed among the industries : Fig. 5 .  The business innovation Source: Manly, J. et. al. (2017) Which innovation model is right for your company? BCG. IKEA Russia operates within the Materials and Retail industry. So that while implementing the innovation for its business, the prior attention must be paid on the internal  processes'  innovation with the help of the  advanced  analytic s . According to BCG research it will assist the business in the optimization of the value chain. Another field for the development is the new product innovation that will increase the loyalty of existing customers and will attract new ones. However, sustainable innovations  may proclaim the other  additional  ways to keep  and accelerate  the growth of IKEA Russia . It is  also  essential to mention about another innovation type that is the strategy innovation. According   to   the   article introduced in HBR,  often  the companies find it difficult to implement innovation and maintain their capabilities to innovate. The main reason behind it is the absence of innovation strategy that is needed to be developed for the alignment of the innovation types with the core business strategy. The innovation strategy supports companies in the development of the consistent innovation system   (Pisano, 2015) .  IKEA has the well-established “People & Planet Positive” strategy that inspire all divisions to introduce the sustainable innovations. The peculiarities of the innovations are that they always require the technological improvements and they are aimed to the enhancement in strategies and operational initiatives. It means that the experience and capability to innovate within the firm can become the powerful basis for the sustainable innovation. However, the innovation in the sustainability field requires taking into consideration not only economic values, but also social and environmental ones. Therefore, it may  increase the challenges for business, since it requires more integrated thinking, because sustainability innovation must be highly connected to all business parts. So that it is important to evaluate the innovation in sustainability field in details. Sustainable innovations  or sustainability-oriented innovations  (SOI ) were firstly mentioned as the technological  solutions to strive  with  ecological challenges.  According to managers , who primarily introduced the sustainable innovations ,  the  compelled  introdu ction of these innovation types  was forced by the legislation and was  no t considered as a  cost efficient for the  business.   However, Porter and Van Der Linde argued that the company can receive the competitive advantage from SOI implementation as it enhance s the  reputation  of the business   and also  produces economic efficiencies.  Nowadays, the perspective on SOI is evolved, since companies implement them proactively, adopting responsible practices to affect  economic, societal  and  environmental dimensions  simultaneously . The framework that unites these dimensions is called Triple Bottom  Line  concept; and it  defines  the ability of  companies  to create greater benefits  (Network for Business Sustainability, 2012) . According to the detailed research of sustainable innovation within the businesses, Network for Business Sustainability  (2012)  has indicated three main context s , in which sustainable innovations  mainly develop , which are the O perat ional Optimization, Organizational Transformation and Systems B uilding  contexts .  Operational O ptimization context  occurs  when the firm introduces the incremental innovation s , which are not evaluated as radical ones, since most  often,  they are just reinventing the existing  traditional  innovation s  toward  sustainable ones . Comparing to the  Operational O ptimization , the context of Systems B uildings implies  innovations connected to  the disruptive and radical changes of the business .  The context of the O rganizatio nal T ransformation is the transition between these t w o contexts.  The introduction of sus tainable innovation within the S ystems  B uildings  context is evaluated as  an  increasingly sustainable  one.  Based on these contexts, the framework for sustainability-oriented innovations was elaborated. The  framework  below, developed by the researchers illustrates ,  which innovation activities business  tends to implement  in each of the  indicated  contexts: Fig. 6 .  Categories of Innovation activities in the three contexts of sustainability-oriented innovation. Source: Network for Business Sustainability (2012). Innovating for sustainability . The left-sided axis  defines  how the company consider s  itself within the system of externalities ,  whether it  is  the part of the system  ( systematic )  or it is an outstanding and independent   section  ( insular ) . The axis on the top illustrates  the internal situation of  the company, determining whether sustainable innovations are stand alone or they are highly correlated with the strategy, vision, business model and processes of the organization  ( integrated ) . And the  right -sided axis determines  the extent to which the company  is able to  introduce  the s ocio-technical innovation s , which impact both organizational and social factor s  within and outside the  borders of a company.   In the way to change the focus   from  Operational Optimization to Organizational T ransformation  based on  the business  needs : to become more systematic, focusing on the implementation of socially and  environmentally oriented  values, which will generate the economic value for the organization; to implement innovations, which are getting more integrated within the whole business rather than being stand-alone, aligning the sustainability-oriented innovations with the core business  strategy. to introduce innovations,  which will include  both technical and social  considerations . It means  that the firm  needs  to develop the technologies that will bring the societal values, inspiring the change not only in the level of business model, but also in the challenging institutional level. However, when the business is going toward Systems Building, the significant influence should be given to the reconsideration of the company’s purpose and consequently the  redesign of  strategy, based on the concept of Triple Bottom Line. Innovations in Systems Building context are developing in the way to inspire the sustainab le development  within the whole system  inside and  outside the borders of the company. It implies that the  organizations need  to i ntroduce the incentives , which can encourage  the transition toward sustainability among all company’s stakeholders.  The framework was introduced in the way to evaluate the sustainable innovation management and current company’s position and its activities. Based on the analysis of the  various researches and  best practices, the authors of the illustrated model have proposed the detailed plan of  innov ations , which is needed  to  be  implement ed  in each  of the presented context.  So that the business is able to compare its current practices with the proposed ones.  In fact, t he proposed innovations by the Network for Business Sustainability are highly interrelated with the traditional innovations developed by BCG, which were indicated earlier. It proves the fact that the sustainability-based innovations are derived from the traditional innovations’ types . The given framework will be   significantly useful  in the way to evaluate the  current  sustainabl e innovations of IKEA in Russia.
