IKEA sells the sorted waste to the company’s partners,  that recycle it into the new materials and sells back some of them, such as cardboard to IKEA company, so that it can introduce the new products from the recycled cardboard . By that initiative IKEA  illustrates its circular economy model of business, since it  gives the second life for  the utilized   materials , inspiring other people to  take the   conscious responsibility to the consumerism and minimize  the waste generation .   The program  makes  the positive social and environmental impact as well as the economic  one, since  the business earn the profit from the  sales of the  utili zed materials.   Nowadays ,  Pravila   Deleniya  program received high popularity among Saint Petersburg citizens, so that IKEA business is working on the expan sion of  Pravila   Deleniya  stations among other  Russian  cities, where IKEA is represented.   Considering Take back furniture program, as it was mentioned before, it is  broadly  used  by IKEA and non-IKEA customers , since it gives the second life for products, minimizing the waste .   Moreover, the program increases the customers’ loyalty and attracts new clients into IKEA stores, since the people participated in that program receive 5% sale for the next purchase in IKEA.  However, these two initiatives do  not cove r the availability to recycle other IKEA products. According to the internal review of IKEA Russia, one of the danger ous IKEA products   as the waste   in the post- utilization stage is IKEA  spring mattresses.  In addition to this it is evaluated as the most challenging  product  to dispose, since  the standard  garbage bins and   garbage trucks are not designed to handle bulky items . Moreover , mattresses have  t he high chance of flammability , m ean ing  that  if  the utilized mattresses are   thrown  away  next to the local yard trash can s ,  and some small spark appears nearby  it , mattre ss can become the reason of the massive fire.   Furthermore,  the leaving the utilized matrasses ne arby  the garbage bins is  evaluated as  the ad ministrative offense.  In addition to this, the fabrics and inner fillers of mattresses are often made from the materials, which emit harmful toxic substances, when they are burned.   Thus, the primary emphasis of the proposed  recommendation  is  the innovat ive program , which will be aimed toward the implementation of circulari ty principles into IKEA mattresses. The company can develop the service of “Take back mat tresses”, which will be aimed to the gathering the utilized mattresses of IKEA and non-IKEA production.  Afterwards, the mattresses will  be  fraction ed   into the spare parts  that are in  turn will  be  recycled  into row materials.  The initiative c an be evaluated as highly sustainable one.  However, since the mattresses are produced from many types of materials and the   composition of each IKEA mattress varies significantly, the recyclability process may not be performed by  only  IKEA Russia own effort s .  In the way to be viable, the following innovation should be developed with the support of the external partners.  Therefore, I KEA needs  to find the business, that have the high  level of  expertise in the  production or in the  recycling  of  mattresses . According to the research of the recycling market,  there is no specialized company  around Russia , which will be specialized on the  mattresses crushing and recycling.  So that IKEA Russia may consider the partnership development with the  services that offers the mattresses utilization.  There are companies, which exist   in Russian market, such as   Utilmobil ’ and  U tilservice ,  which are specializ ing  on the mattresses' utilization. However, after the consultation with the specialists of these companies on the hot line, it was revealed that the c ompanies  are able to  collect the old mattresses from  the  households and   just to  move them to  a   garbage dump. It can be useful for  the convenience of people ;  however, it is highly unsustainable  due to the fact that  the products are still  not  recycled,  and they still produce the toxic gasses after burring in dumps. Afterwards, I have evaluated the other   companies , which produce mattresses  in Russia  and  offers the service of mattresses utilization  in the way to analyze the proposed service .   The logic  to choose  only Russian mattresses producers was connected to the recycling possibilities: if the company produce s  th e mattresses locally, it may imply the fact that the  enterprise  recycle s  the mattress and receives the spare part, which can be used for the following production. The innovation will be tightly connected to the launch of the program of electric vehicles, which was announced at the end of  2019. IKEA Russia is the first retail company in Russian market that introduced the innovative delivery using the electric vehicles,  which are striving not only to minimize the costs for the petrol, but primarily to lessen the ecological impact from the logistics processes.  According to the interview with IKEA sustainable manager, nowadays the company has  some  insignificant  number of electric vehicles in auto park. However, the  replacing all traditional cars with electric ones is still a big challenge for a business, since there is a poor infrastructure in big Russian cities. The infrastructure here implies the fact that there is a poor nu mber of charging stations for cars available around the city.  Thus, what IKEA can do is to establish the charging station in the  own parking spaces, which will give a further opportunity for a business to utilize more electric vehicles with out any obstacles, connected with charging  process.  The innovation implies the  Systems Building contexts, since it gives the new sustainable opportunities not only for the company, but also for IKEA customers and citizens . The innovation makes the charging process more available and easier, since customers have an opportunity to recharge their cars, while they have shopping.  Thus, during the daylight hours,  the citizens can charge their car, while during the night hours the charging stations may be utilized by corporate electric vehicles of IKEA. Thus, the  following sustainable  inn ovation will become feasible  both in the short-term and long-term perspectives. The risk assessment  of the proposed recommendations In the way to  evaluate  the  viability of the proposed recommendations as the sustainable innovations the assessment of the risks for each recommendation should be performed. In the way to perform it, the Risk Scenario Matrix toll will be utilized. It will  evaluate each risks, based on two main criteria: (1) The impact of the risk and (2) The likelihood of a risk. So that, the following  chapter is aimed to identify the urgency and severance of each risk for the developed recommendations in the way that the business will be able to  mitigate them in future perspectives. Afterwards, the  Risk Scenario Matrix will be represented.   The  introduction  of the circular economy initiatives for broader range of products in IKEA.  The risk that Free-Util’ company will  decline the proposal of IKEA to be contracted on the proposed conditions .   As this company is unique in Russian market,  as  the business that utilizes the mattresses in the sustainable way, sending the spare parts for the recycling,   the  risk is cons idered  as the risk with high impact  (in a significant way) , since IKEA needs to implement sustainable innovations as soon as possible.   However,  it is  relatively unlikely, since  with the support of furniture giant IKEA, Free-util will be  broadly promoted and will receive the additional revenue stream.   Otherwise, as the solution for this risk  mitigation,  IKEA should communicate to Free-util business , that it can  become flexible in the  contracts’ condition s  development.   The risk that customers of IKEA Russia will not be able to send the predicted number of mattresses for the recycling into Free-util service.  The risk is very unlikely  due to the fact that  IKEA will promote the new sustainable innovations. Moreover, according to the interview with sustainable managers, the internal survey of IKEA indicated that Russian citizens request even bigger number of sustainable initiatives. Considering the impact of that the risk can bring to the company, it is moderate, since in that case IKEA will lose some part of potential commission fee from Free-util company. Furthermore, Free-util may consider the partnership with IKEA as not essential one and due to the poor number of the old mattresses taken from clients may break the contract. The expansion of the charging stations for electric vehicles in IKEA parking space with the support of charging stations . The risk that IKEA  Russia   will not receive the subsidized support from the government for the establishment of charging stations.  The following  risk is very likely in regards of  its   probability since  the government may have already developed the investment planning  for the development the inf rastructure for electric vehicles.   Nevertheless, the risk  is  insignificant, since IKEA Russia will be able to  cover the costs  of the charging stations   by own  financial abilities   (since the investments can be covered very quickly by the minimization of petrol usage). The restriction  of the  internal budget of IKEA for the purchase of th e   electric vehicles . This risk can directly influence on  payback of the initiative. It means, that if IKEA Russia will have  no new electric vehicles in auto park, th is  investment will not generate th e   ability for a business to generate the costs ’ optimization .  Considering the likelihood of the  risk  has  unlikely status, since according to the interview with the manager of IKEA, the company has th e great plans for the purchase of additional  electric vehicles, which will in turn require  the charging stations. In regards of the impact, it is m inor , since even though IKEA will have minimum number of electric cars, the indirect profit would be generated by the new customers, who decided to use an opportunity of charging station  in IKEA parking space.   T he development of the additional small-sized production based on the collaboration with social entrepreneurs The poor quality of the  final goods produced in a new production point.   According to the impact,  it is  significant, since IKEA promotes the international standards for goods and communicates that the quality of  its products is  really high . So that if the company  is not  able to  match to the promises and  does not  fulfill the  requests from customers, it can  lose  the reputation and, therefore, its market share.  In regards of the likelihood, it is conditionally likely, since the  employees can be not aware about the  high-quality  standards and just have no  experience working in such conditions, which are aimed to support the high quality of products. So that, to mitigate the risk, IKEA Russia must  develop the special inductions , so that all employees of a new production will be able to  match with the identified standards. The risk  of poorly qualified labor force in the region of production.  In regards of the level of impact this risk is evaluated as an insignificant one, since  the  innovation will be devoted as  the long-term  initiative. So that, before the hiring, IKEA  have the  long period, and therefore it   may develop the survey of the labor market, and if the qualification of a labor force will be poor, the business may develop the specified  courses for the motivated candidates, who will be able to  develop their hard skills before the  start day.  In regards of the likelihood,  it the risk is unlikely, since the  work  implies the manual labor, connected to the daily routines  of  majority women in small regions, who have low level of income to spend additional money for the  tailoring service . So, to sum up the risks identified above, t he risks Scenario Matrix is provided below, which evaluates  each risk according to its likelihood and  impact.  Fig. 7 . Risks Scenario Matrix for IKEA Russia sustainable innovations Source: adapted from Project Management Academy (n.d.) As it can be seen, the major risks are related to the recommendation in regards of the mattresses recycling program, (risks 1 and 2) and the other one is related to the development of a new small-sized production in Russian region (risk 5). Therefore,  in the way to mitigate risks,  the  recommendations mentioned nearby each  risk   have to  be applied by IKEA Russia before the implementation of sustainable initiatives in life. Conclusion  of a final qualification paper The following qualification paper is a consultancy project, which was aimed to provide the appropriate recommendations for IKEA business in Russian market in the field of sustainable development. The given theme of the paper was developed  because  nowadays the company has essential challenges in the introduction of its global sustainable objectives. These objectives are identified in the agenda of IKEA global strategy for sustainable development, called People & Planet Positive. IKEA is proactively introducing the innovative projects for a sustainable future, and even  has achieved the great results and the high scoring among the “green” companies in Russia. However, the business still faces issues, connected to the peculiarities of Russian market, so that it is considerably hard for a business to commit to its global strategy due to the obstacles in Russia. Thus, the relevance of the topic  is connected with  the real managerial problem, which is derived from the challenges of the implementation of the sustainable initiatives in Russia, which are demanded by the international IKEA sustainability strategy.  So that in the way to solve the managerial problem and to develop the proposals for the viable business recommendations, which can be both beneficial for global sustainability agenda and attractive according to the Triple Bottom Line for IKEA business in Russia, the various analysis was conducted, such as: Evaluation of a s trategic positioning and sustainable orientation of a comp any; Asses sment of t he initiatives  of sustainable development strategy  globally and in Russia ; The  extended v alue Chain analysis through the prism of sustainable development; The  sustainability-based analysis  of the external environment  (PESTEL-analysis) with the application  to the furniture retail industry Evaluation of current  initiatives of IKEA in Russia  through the prism  of sustainable innovations. The research analysis  identified above gave me an opportunity to develop the map of the SWOT-analysis, which was elaborated with the application on to the sustainable development  agenda , based on the identified internal and external insights in regards of sustainability. The development of the detailed SWOT-analysis became the basis for the identification of the  recommendations, which can become the most applicable for a business in such a way that IKEA Russia will be able to successfully commit to the global People & Planet Positive strategy. Concluding everything mentioned above,  based on the thorough analysis, three sustainable innovations were targeted toward the fulfillment of  the goals from  three key areas of the  sustainable IKEA strategy.  So that,  the following  was recommended for IKEA  in Russian market : The expansion of the circular economy initiatives for broader range of products in IKEA . The innovation will be targeted toward the  introduction of a “Take back mattresses” program , which will be developed  with the collaboration of Free-util company, which will help IKEA customers to recycle the utilized mattresses in a sustainable  manner . The expansion of the charging stations for electric vehicles in IKEA parking space  w ith the support of charging stations’  provider called Simple Electric  and with the possible  subsidized financial support  receive d  from the governmental (as  IKEA company in Russia   aims to  develop the  infrastructure for  the market of  electric vehicles). The development of the additional small-sized production based on the collaboration with social entrepreneurs, which implies the launch of the manufacturing in Yaroslavl’ region that will be able to provide IKEA stores with bed linen, tablecloth and curtains. It will give an opportunity for the locals to have an additional opportunity for work. Afterwards, the risks’ assessment was performed with the tool of Risk Scenario matrix, which gave an opportunity for the evaluation of possibilities of risks for each recommendation in the way to identify their significance and the impact on the company’s performance. To sum up, the qualification paper  was achieved its key goal, which was  the development of the solution for the identified managerial problem within IKEA Russia, based on the thorough analysis.
